rulings.Case Title: JDF HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL VS. QBE INSURANCE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 02, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANT QBE INSURANCE CORPORATION's MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER Trial. The motion to bifurcate is TRANSFERRED to Department 206 on the day of trial, June 2, 2025, to be heard by the assigned trial judge. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN KHOURI-HADDAD VS. GARY M. LEVIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Motion To Compel (1) Deposition Of Plaintiff; (2) Production Of Documents At Deposition; (3) Execution Of An Authorization For The Release Of Mental Health Records; (4) Answers To Questions At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 02, 2025, Line 8. DEFENDANT CABLE CAR HOTEL, LLC.'s Motion To Compel (1) Deposition Of Plaintiff; (2) Production Of Documents At Deposition; (3) Execution Of An Authorization For The Release Of Mental Health Records; (4) Answers To Questions At Deposition. Defendant Cable Car Hotel, LLC's Motion To Compel Motion To Compel (1) Deposition Of Plaintiff; (2) Production Of Documents At Deposition; (3) Execution Of An Authorization For The Release Of Mental Health Records; (4) Answers To Questions At Deposition is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 02, 2025, Line 11. DEFENDANTs LEGALMATCH.COM, DMITRY SHUBOV, and VLADIMIR SHUBOV's Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff'S Deposition. This motion is continued to May 12, 2025, to be transferred to the appointed discovery referee. All parties are put on notice that the court is continuing the discovery motions in this case to May 12, 2025, not May 2, 2025, as stated in the April 23, 2025, order. rulings.Case Title: KIERAN FARR VS. CHRISTIAN ISAM BABA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deponent Christian Isam Baba To Produce Documents Demanded By The Deposition Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 02, 2025, Line 10. PLAINTIFF KIERAN FARR's Motion To Compel Deponent Christian Isam Baba To Produce Documents Demanded By The Deposition Notice. Plaintiff Kieran Farr's Motion to Compel Deponent Christian Isam Baba to Produce Documents Demanded by the Deposition Notice is DENIED. On April 9, 2025, Plaintiff served a Second Amended Notice of Deposition that does not include a production demand. (Ganapathy Decl., par. 7, Ex. F.) That second amended notice supersedes the prior notices and the production demand is now moot. The court further notes that the deposition occurred on April 16, 2025, and Defendant produced the documents. (Id. at pars. 8-9; Ex. H.) While the court is not convinced Plaintiff acted with substantial justification as to discovery, the court finds the overall circumstances make sanction inappropriate. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
